en.extract:
The Javan bush warbler (Locustella montis) is a songbird species. Formerly placed in the "Old World warbler" assemblage, it is now placed in the newly recognized family Locustellidae.
It is found in the mountains of eastern Java, Bali, Timor and Alor Island.
It is an insectivore.

The '''Javan bush warbler''' ('''''Locustella montis''''') is a [[songbird]] [[species]]. Formerly placed in the "[[Old World warbler]]" assemblage, it is now placed in the newly recognized [[Family (taxonomy)|family]] [[Locustellidae]].

It is found in the mountains of eastern [[Java]], [[Bali]], [[Timor]] and [[Alor Island]].

It is an insectivore.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Nurrofik |first=Agus |last2=Fathoni |first2=Muhammad |last3=Kurnianto |first3=Agung Sih |last4=Septiadi |first4=Luhur |last5=Kurniawan |first5=Nia |date=2021-02-10 |title=Avifaunal Diversity and Community Structure in Universitas Brawijaya Forest, East Java, Indonesia |journal=Journal of Tropical Biodiversity and Biotechnology |volume=6 |issue=1 |article-number=58335 |doi=10.22146/jtbb.58335 |issn=2540-9581}}</ref>
